| **Succeeded by** | | |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | [8th Dynasty of Egypt](/wiki/8th_Dynasty_of_Egypt "8th Dynasty of Egypt") | |  | [10th Dynasty of Egypt](/wiki/10th_Dynasty_of_Egypt "10th Dynasty of Egypt") | | |  |  | | --- | --- | | [12th Dynasty of Egypt](/wiki/12th_Dynasty_of_Egypt "12th Dynasty of Egypt") |  | | | |

of [ancient Egypt](/wiki/Ancient_Egypt "Ancient Egypt") (notated **Dynasty XI**; c. 2150 BC – c. 1991 BC) is a well-attested group of rulers. Its earlier members before King [Mentuhotep II](/wiki/Mentuhotep_II "Mentuhotep II") are grouped with the four preceding [dynasties](/wiki/Dynasty "Dynasty") to form the [First Intermediate Period](/wiki/First_Intermediate_Period "First Intermediate Period"), whereas the later members are considered part of the [Middle Kingdom](/wiki/Middle_Kingdom_of_Egypt "Middle Kingdom of Egypt"). They all ruled from [Thebes](/wiki/Thebes_(Egypt) "Thebes (Egypt)") in Upper Egypt.

The **11th Dynasty** of [ancient Egypt](/wiki/Ancient_Egypt "Ancient Egypt") (notated **Dynasty XI**; c. 2150 BC – c. 1991 BC) is a well-attested group of rulers. Its earlier members before King [Mentuhotep II](/wiki/Mentuhotep_II "Mentuhotep II") are grouped with the four preceding [dynasties](/wiki/Dynasty "Dynasty") to form the [First Intermediate Period](/wiki/First_Intermediate_Period "First Intermediate Period"), whereas the later members are considered part of the [Middle Kingdom](/wiki/Middle_Kingdom_of_Egypt "Middle Kingdom of Egypt"). They all ruled from [Thebes](/wiki/Thebes_(Egypt) "Thebes (Egypt)") in Upper Egypt.

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eleventh_Dynasty_of_Egypt&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

The relative chronology of the 11th Dynasty is well established by contemporary attestations and, except for count Intef and Mentuhotep IV, by the [Turin canon](/wiki/Turin_King_List "Turin King List").[[1]](#cite_note-schneider-1)

[Manetho](/wiki/Manetho "Manetho")'s statement that the 11th Dynasty consisted of 16 kings, who reigned for 43 years is contradicted by contemporary inscriptions and the evidence of the [Turin King List](/wiki/Turin_King_List "Turin King List"), whose combined testimony establishes that this kingdom consisted of seven kings who ruled for a total of 143 years.[[2]](#cite_note-2) However, his testimony that this dynasty was based at [Thebes](/wiki/Thebes,_Egypt "Thebes, Egypt") is verified by the contemporary evidence. It was during this dynasty that all of [ancient Egypt](/wiki/Ancient_Egypt "Ancient Egypt") was united under the [Middle Kingdom](/wiki/Middle_Kingdom_of_Egypt "Middle Kingdom of Egypt").

This dynasty traces its origins to a [nomarch](/wiki/Nomarch "Nomarch") of Thebes, "[Intef the Great](/wiki/Intef_the_Elder "Intef the Elder"), son of Iku",[[1]](#cite_note-schneider-1) who is mentioned in a number of contemporary inscriptions. However, his immediate successor [Mentuhotep I](/wiki/Mentuhotep_I "Mentuhotep I") is considered the first king of this dynasty.

An inscription carved during the reign of Wahankh [Intef II](/wiki/Intef_II "Intef II") shows that he was the first of this dynasty to claim to rule over the whole of Egypt, a claim which brought the Thebans into conflict with the [10th-Dynasty rulers](/wiki/10th_Dynasty "10th Dynasty") of [Herakleopolis Magna](/wiki/Herakleopolis_Magna "Herakleopolis Magna"). Intef undertook several campaigns northwards, and captured the important nome of [Abydos](/wiki/Abydos,_Egypt "Abydos, Egypt").

Warfare continued intermittently between the Theban and Heracleapolitan dynasts until shortly before the 39th [regnal year](/wiki/Regnal_year "Regnal year") of Nebhepetre [Mentuhotep II](/wiki/Mentuhotep_II "Mentuhotep II"), when the Herakleopolitans were defeated, and this dynasty could begin to consolidate their rule. The rulers of the 11th Dynasty reasserted Egypt's influence over her neighbors in [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa") and the Near East. Mentuhotep II sent renewed expeditions to [Phoenicia](/wiki/Phoenicia "Phoenicia") to obtain [cedar](/wiki/Cedrus_libani "Cedrus libani"). Sankhkare [Mentuhotep III](/wiki/Mentuhotep_III "Mentuhotep III") sent an expedition from [Coptos](/wiki/Coptos "Coptos") south to the [land of Punt](/wiki/Land_of_Punt "Land of Punt").

The reign of its last king, and thus the end of this dynasty, is something of a mystery. Contemporary records refer to "seven empty years" following the death of Mentuhotep III, which correspond to the reign of Nebtawyre [Mentuhotep IV](/wiki/Mentuhotep_IV "Mentuhotep IV"). Modern scholars identify his [vizier](/wiki/Vizier "Vizier") Amenemhat with [Amenemhat I](/wiki/Amenemhat_I "Amenemhat I"), the first king of [12th Dynasty](/wiki/12th_Dynasty "12th Dynasty"), as part of a theory that Amenemhat became king as part of a palace coup. The only certain details of Mentuhotep's reign was that two remarkable omens were witnessed at the quarry of [Wadi Hammamat](/wiki/Wadi_Hammamat "Wadi Hammamat") by the vizier Amenemhat. Nobody knows for certain how [Amenemhat I](/wiki/Amenemhat_I "Amenemhat I") rose to power and became the next king.

## Rulers of the 11th Dynasty

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eleventh_Dynasty_of_Egypt&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Rulers of the 11th Dynasty")]

Initially the rulers of the 11th Dynasty were only Nomarchs but starting during the reign of Intef I they declared themselves pharaohs

## Comparison of regnal lists

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eleventh_Dynasty_of_Egypt&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Comparison of regnal lists")]

Kings [Mentuhotep II](/wiki/Mentuhotep_II "Mentuhotep II") and [Mentuhotep III](/wiki/Mentuhotep_III "Mentuhotep III") are the most commonly included kings of this dynasty to appear on Egyptian king lists, while their successor [Mentuhotep IV](/wiki/Mentuhotep_IV "Mentuhotep IV") was often ignored. The [Karnak King List](/wiki/Karnak_King_List "Karnak King List") provides the most complete surviving list of names for the eleventh dynasty, beginning with the nomarch [Intef the Elder](/wiki/Intef_the_Elder "Intef the Elder"), whose name is the only one on this list that does not have a [cartouche](/wiki/Cartouche "Cartouche") because he never reigned as Pharaoh, and ending with Mentuhotep IV, one of the few lists to include him. Several names are lost and damaged, but can still be reconstructed.

The [Turin King List](/wiki/Turin_King_List "Turin King List") begins this dynasty with [Mentuhotep I](/wiki/Mentuhotep_I "Mentuhotep I") and provides reign lengths for individual kings, however it is in a fragmentary state and some information is now lost. Mentuhotep IV is not mentioned at all, but the list does note "a [lacuna](/wiki/Lacuna_(manuscripts) "Lacuna (manuscripts)") of 7 years" following Mentuhotep III.[[3]](#cite_note-Turin-3)

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Intef the Elder](/wiki/Intef_the_Elder "Intef the Elder") | Intef | – | – | – | – |
| [Mentuhotep I](/wiki/Mentuhotep_I "Mentuhotep I") | Men[tuhotep] | – | – | *Name lost* | *Lost* |
| [Intef I](/wiki/Intef_I "Intef I") | In[tef] | – | – | *Name lost* | *Lost* |
| [Intef II](/wiki/Intef_II "Intef II") | Intef | – | – | [I]n[tef] | 49 years |
| [Intef III](/wiki/Intef_III "Intef III") | *Name lost* | – | – | *Name lost* | 8 years |
| [Mentuhotep II](/wiki/Mentuhotep_II "Mentuhotep II") | Nebhepetre | Nebhepetre | Nebhepetre | Nebhapetre | 51 years |
| [Mentuhotep III](/wiki/Mentuhotep_III "Mentuhotep III") | Seneferkare | Sankhkare | Seankhkare | Sankhka(re) | 12 years |
| [Mentuhotep IV](/wiki/Mentuhotep_IV "Mentuhotep IV") | [Nebtawy]re | – | – | *Omitted* | 7 years |
